question: Which component transmits power from the transmission to the drive axle(s)?

answers.explanation: The clutch assembly transmits power from the engine to the transmission. The drive shaft then transmits power to the drive axles. A drive shaft loop is a safety device that prevents the driveshaft from dropping onto the road if a shaft or a universal joint breaks.

explanation: The clutch assembly transmits power from the engine to the transmission. The drive shaft then transmits power to the drive axles. A drive shaft loop is a safety device that prevents the driveshaft from dropping onto the road if a shaft or a universal joint breaks.

question: Which component converts rotational steering wheel action into axial (lateral) movement at the front wheels?

answers.explanation: On a traditional steering system (not rack and pinion), the steering column attaches the steering wheel to the steering gear box. The box converts rotational movement to axial movement that causes the front wheels to move left or right.

explanation: On a traditional steering system (not rack and pinion), the steering column attaches the steering wheel to the steering gear box. The box converts rotational movement to axial movement that causes the front wheels to move left or right.

question: What is the largest portion of the fifth wheel and must be inspected for cracks, breaks, and requires lubrication?

answers.explanation: The largest portion of the fifth wheel is the top plate or skid plate. This is the portion that the trailer slides (skids) onto during coupling. It must be inspected for cracks, breaks, and needs to be kept lubricated.

explanation: The largest portion of the fifth wheel is the top plate or skid plate. This is the portion that the trailer slides (skids) onto during coupling. It must be inspected for cracks, breaks, and needs to be kept lubricated.

question: Which component (when equipped) is located within the slide mechanism of a fifth wheel and should not be loose, nor missing?

answers.explanation: Not every fifth wheel design has locking pins. When equipped, make sure they are not loose or missing.

explanation: Not every fifth wheel design has locking pins. When equipped, make sure they are not loose or missing.

question: Which wheel components should show no signs of being loose, rusted, missing, or having damaged threads?

answers.explanation: Check each wheel to ensure that all of the lugs are present, tight, and have no signs of being loose, rusted, or damaged threads.

explanation: Check each wheel to ensure that all of the lugs are present, tight, and have no signs of being loose, rusted, or damaged threads.

question: A sight glass found protruding from the center hub of a wheel allows you to check the level of:

answers.explanation: The sight glass is mounted to the end of the axle as part of the hub and extends through the center of the wheel. This sight glass allows you to check the level of the gear lube, or hub oil, in the axle.

explanation: The sight glass is mounted to the end of the axle as part of the hub and extends through the center of the wheel. This sight glass allows you to check the level of the gear lube, or hub oil, in the axle.

question: During a pre-trip inspection, it is important that these components are not rubbing against tractor parts, nor are they pinched, tangled, or worn.

answers.explanation: The air and electrical lines should not be rubbing anywhere. They should be free of wear, tangles and not be pinched.

explanation: The air and electrical lines should not be rubbing anywhere. They should be free of wear, tangles and not be pinched.

question: Which components transmit brake pedal pressure to the brake drums/shoes using air or brake fluid?

answers.explanation: The metal brake lines and the air hoses between the tractor and trailer(s) transmit pedal pressure to the brake assemblies to apply the brakes.

explanation: The metal brake lines and the air hoses between the tractor and trailer(s) transmit pedal pressure to the brake assemblies to apply the brakes.

question: Which component is used to disengage the locking jaws of the fifth wheel during coupling?

answers.explanation: The release arm is used to disengage the locking jaws whether coupling or uncoupling. The latch must be lubricated and operate smoothly.

explanation: The release arm is used to disengage the locking jaws whether coupling or uncoupling. The latch must be lubricated and operate smoothly.

question: Which type of mechanism is used to engage and hold the kingpin on a tandem axle trailer?

answers.text: The tandem release arm with locking pins
answers.explanation: The tandem release arm with locking pins, if so equipped, would be the slide mechanism to engage and hold the kingpin on a tandem trailer.

explanation: The tandem release arm with locking pins, if so equipped, would be the slide mechanism to engage and hold the kingpin on a tandem trailer.

question: All the following are responsible for maintaining air brake system pressure, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: The air compressor, driven by the engine, generates the compressed air. The governor and a pressure control system manage how much pressure is in the system.

explanation: The air compressor, driven by the engine, generates the compressed air. The governor and a pressure control system manage how much pressure is in the system.

question: With the engine running and at operating temperature, you notice a small puddle of water under the truck. What is the most likely cause?

answers.text: Condensation from the air conditioner running
answers.explanation: Leaking engine coolant will have a sweet smell and appear green or orange. Leaking power steering will look like oil and be red or dark colored. Condensation on the ground from running the cab air conditioner is normal.

explanation: Leaking engine coolant will have a sweet smell and appear green or orange. Leaking power steering will look like oil and be red or dark colored. Condensation on the ground from running the cab air conditioner is normal.

question: All the following are responsible for making the vehicle ride as smooth as possible, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: The leaf springs (or coil springs) absorb the roughness of the road and smooth out the ride. The shock absorbers remove the bouncing (jounce and rebound) that can be caused by the springs. The axles do not affect ride quality.

explanation: The leaf springs (or coil springs) absorb the roughness of the road and smooth out the ride. The shock absorbers remove the bouncing (jounce and rebound) that can be caused by the springs. The axles do not affect ride quality.

question: Which component(s) must be secure, operating smoothly, and undamaged to improve visibility during inclement weather?

answers.explanation: The windshield glass must be undamaged. The wipers must be secure, undamaged, and operate smoothly to ensure good visibility during inclement weather conditions.

explanation: The windshield glass must be undamaged. The wipers must be secure, undamaged, and operate smoothly to ensure good visibility during inclement weather conditions.

question: Which component is attached to the tractor frame and must be clear of obstructions and securely fastened?

answers.explanation: The catwalk must be securely fastened to the tractor frame and have no obstructions that would prevent you from passing.

explanation: The catwalk must be securely fastened to the tractor frame and have no obstructions that would prevent you from passing.

question: Which component is responsible for transmitting engine power to the transmission?

answers.explanation: The clutch assembly will transmit power from the engine to the transmission.

explanation: The clutch assembly will transmit power from the engine to the transmission.

question: Which component must not have any illegal stickers, damage, or obstructions, while being kept clean?

answers.explanation: The mirrors and vehicle windshield must be kept clean, free from obstructions, damage, or illegal stickers.

explanation: The mirrors and vehicle windshield must be kept clean, free from obstructions, damage, or illegal stickers.

question: Which components are used to attach the leaf springs to the trailer and axles?

answers.explanation: The leaf springs are located between the trailer (or tractor frame) and the axle. Spring hangers and mounts are used to fasten them to each.

explanation: The leaf springs are located between the trailer (or tractor frame) and the axle. Spring hangers and mounts are used to fasten them to each.

question: The tires on which portion of the vehicle must have a tread depth of at least 4/32 inch?

answers.explanation: The tires on wheels that steer the vehicle (steering axle or independent suspension) need to have a minimum of at least 4/32 inch of tread.

explanation: The tires on wheels that steer the vehicle (steering axle or independent suspension) need to have a minimum of at least 4/32 inch of tread.

question: Which component(s) should be in place on the door to reduce air, water, and noise leaks?

answers.explanation: All seals should be in place to eliminate air, water, and noise leaks.

explanation: All seals should be in place to eliminate air, water, and noise leaks.

question: Which gauge on the instrument cluster is related to engine health?

answers.explanation: Engine oil pressure provides the most accurate indication of engine health and needs to be monitored regularly to ensure the truck can keep running.

explanation: Engine oil pressure provides the most accurate indication of engine health and needs to be monitored regularly to ensure the truck can keep running.

question: Which is the following is LEAST likely to be checked using a dipstick?

answers.explanation: Both the engine oil and transmission fluid are likely to have a dipstick. Always ensure they are filled to the appropriate level. Some transmissions are "filled for life" and will not need to be checked. The coolant level will normally be checked using a sight glass, or seeing the level on a semi-transparent overflow bottle.

explanation: Both the engine oil and transmission fluid are likely to have a dipstick. Always ensure they are filled to the appropriate level. Some transmissions are "filled for life" and will not need to be checked. The coolant level will normally be checked using a sight glass, or seeing the level on a semi-transparent overflow bottle.

question: Each component is inspected at the axle or wheel area during a walk around of the vehicle, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: The steering gearbox may be accessed during the engine inspection, or from under the front of the truck. Brakes and leaf springs should be inspected at each axle and wheel.

explanation: The steering gearbox may be accessed during the engine inspection, or from under the front of the truck. Brakes and leaf springs should be inspected at each axle and wheel.

question: During the pre-trip inspection, which brake system needs to be checked to ensure it does not pull to the left or right?

answers.explanation: Drive forward to about 5 mph and apply the brake pedal. The brakes should apply evenly with no pulling. If the brakes pull to one side, the brakes on the side of the pull are working correctly and it is the opposite side that should be inspected.

explanation: Drive forward to about 5 mph and apply the brake pedal. The brakes should apply evenly with no pulling. If the brakes pull to one side, the brakes on the side of the pull are working correctly and it is the opposite side that should be inspected.

question: While testing the exterior lighting systems, which system will NOT have a light on the instrument cluster associated with the test?

answers.explanation: While testing the exterior lighting systems, the turn signal indicators, four-way flashers, and high beam headlights will all have corresponding lights on the instrument cluster. The service brake will not.

explanation: While testing the exterior lighting systems, the turn signal indicators, four-way flashers, and high beam headlights will all have corresponding lights on the instrument cluster. The service brake will not.

question: During the Class A CDL inspection portion of the test, you will need to perform one of _____ different variations of the inspection on the vehicle provided.

answers.explanation: You will be provided a vehicle for the Class A CDL inspection portion of the test. You will be told which version of the four inspections to perform at that time. They include engine start, in-cab inspection, coupling inspection, or full vehicle inspection.

explanation: You will be provided a vehicle for the Class A CDL inspection portion of the test. You will be told which version of the four inspections to perform at that time. They include engine start, in-cab inspection, coupling inspection, or full vehicle inspection.

question: Which component may have more than one, so ensure that none are missing when inspecting the coupling area?

answers.explanation: When equipped, there may be multiple locking pins to secure the kingpin in the fifth wheel coupling. Ensure that none are missing.

explanation: When equipped, there may be multiple locking pins to secure the kingpin in the fifth wheel coupling. Ensure that none are missing.

question: Which statement concerning wheel spacers (Budd spacing) is FALSE?

answers.text: Inspect that the spacers are properly offset between the dual wheels to provide the correct gap.
answers.explanation: Not all wheels will have spacers. When equipped, the spacers should be centered and the dual wheels evenly separated. There should be nothing bent, damaged, or rusted and no debris should be stuck between the dual wheels.

answers.text: If equipped, ensure that the spacers are not bent, damaged, or rusted.
Ensure that no debris or materials from the road have become lodged between the dual wheels.

explanation: Not all wheels will have spacers. When equipped, the spacers should be centered and the dual wheels evenly separated. There should be nothing bent, damaged, or rusted and no debris should be stuck between the dual wheels.

question: Which of the following is NOT included when inspecting drum brake assemblies?

answers.text: Checking for cracks, dents, breakage, or signs of overheating
Ensuring adequate brake shoe lining thickness for safe braking
Measuring the thickness of the brake rotors
answers.explanation: First, brake rotors are used on disk brakes, not drum brake assemblies. Second, measuring rotor thickness would not be done during a walk-around inspection even if the truck had disk brakes, though a technician would make this check during service.

explanation: First, brake rotors are used on disk brakes, not drum brake assemblies. Second, measuring rotor thickness would not be done during a walk-around inspection even if the truck had disk brakes, though a technician would make this check during service.

question: What is the most common method of detecting an air leak in a brake line?

answers.text: Placing the air line under water
Using soap and water to see if air bubbles are produced

answers.explanation: During a walk-around inspection, assuming the air brake system is up to operating pressure, a driver is most likely to hear an air leak at a line. The vehicle must be serviced before driving.

explanation: During a walk-around inspection, assuming the air brake system is up to operating pressure, a driver is most likely to hear an air leak at a line. The vehicle must be serviced before driving.

question: Which vehicle configuration will require splash guards in the state of California

answers.explanation: Within the state of California, vehicles with three or more wheels and every trailer or semitrailer is expected to have fenders, covers, flaps or splash aprons, with a few exceptions for small trailers.

answers.text: A trailer with an unladen weight of 1,000 pounds

explanation: Within the state of California, vehicles with three or more wheels and every trailer or semitrailer is expected to have fenders, covers, flaps or splash aprons, with a few exceptions for small trailers.

question: Which statement is FALSE concerning a CMV seat belt?

answers.text: It must be adjusted to the correct length for the driver.
The latch must operate smoothly and correctly
The driver must have the belt latched anytime they are in the vehicle
answers.explanation: Seat belts must be properly adjusted and in good working order. The belt must be latched anytime the vehicle is moving. Failure to latch a seat belt can cause you to fail your CDL skills test.

explanation: Seat belts must be properly adjusted and in good working order. The belt must be latched anytime the vehicle is moving. Failure to latch a seat belt can cause you to fail your CDL skills test.

question: Which system is tested by gently trying to pull forward to see if the vehicle moves?

answers.explanation: To test the trailer parking brakes, pull forward gently with the tractor to see if the vehicle will move.

explanation: To test the trailer parking brakes, pull forward gently with the tractor to see if the vehicle will move.

question: All the following are checked as part of inspecting the trailer landing gear, EXCEPT:

answers.text: The leaves are not broken, cracked, or missing
answers.explanation: Inspection of the trailer landing gear includes that it fully raises and lowers with no missing or broken parts. The support frame is undamaged and the crank handle is secured. Also, there are no air or hydraulic leaks for powered systems.

answers.text: The support frame is undamaged and crank handle is secured
There are no air or hydraulic leaks for powered systems

explanation: Inspection of the trailer landing gear includes that it fully raises and lowers with no missing or broken parts. The support frame is undamaged and the crank handle is secured. Also, there are no air or hydraulic leaks for powered systems.

question: Which component(s) is most susceptible to developing corrosion, so must be inspected to ensure that it is clean or has minimal corrosion?

answers.explanation: It is common for battery terminals to develop corrosion and start to cause poor connections for the cables. Water with baking soda can quickly eliminate the corrosion. Ideally, the terminal/cable connection area should use dielectric grease to prevent corrosion build-up.

explanation: It is common for battery terminals to develop corrosion and start to cause poor connections for the cables. Water with baking soda can quickly eliminate the corrosion. Ideally, the terminal/cable connection area should use dielectric grease to prevent corrosion build-up.

question: Which component will have a reduced ability to absorb and dissipate heat, resulting in poor brake performance, if it becomes too thin?

answers.explanation: If the brake shoe linings, or the brake drums, become excessively worn they will no longer be able to absorb and dissipate heat quickly enough to support proper braking and begin to fade quicker.

answers.text: The service and emergency air hoses

explanation: If the brake shoe linings, or the brake drums, become excessively worn they will no longer be able to absorb and dissipate heat quickly enough to support proper braking and begin to fade quicker.

question: What is the maximum amount of slack that should exist in a slack adjuster when inspecting the tension of the adjuster?

answers.explanation: When checking the slack adjusters, if they have more than 1 inch of slack, they should be adjusted to be tighter.

explanation: When checking the slack adjusters, if they have more than 1 inch of slack, they should be adjusted to be tighter.

question: What is the proper method of testing the parking brake?

answers.text: Apply the parking brake and then attempt to slowly pull forward
answers.explanation: Apply the parking brake and then attempt to slowly pull forward, the vehicle should not move.

answers.text: Accelerate to about 5 mph and then apply the parking brake to see if the vehicle stops
Apply and release the parking brake several times and monitor the air tanks for any leak down

explanation: Apply the parking brake and then attempt to slowly pull forward, the vehicle should not move.

question: During a Class B or C CDL inspection test, you will need to perform one of _____ different variations of the inspection on the vehicle provided.

answers.explanation: The inspection portion of the test for CDL B or C will require to you complete one inspection out of a possible three scenarios. Your evaluator will inform you of which inspection to complete when the test begins.

explanation: The inspection portion of the test for CDL B or C will require to you complete one inspection out of a possible three scenarios. Your evaluator will inform you of which inspection to complete when the test begins.

question: Regarding instrument cluster gauges, which gauge should slowly and gradually increase rather than a quick and sudden increase to the correct reading?

answers.explanation: Temperature gauges such as engine oil and engine coolant temperature will slowly and gradually increase as the engine warms to operating temperature. Engine RPM and oil pressure will provide an almost instant reading.

explanation: Temperature gauges such as engine oil and engine coolant temperature will slowly and gradually increase as the engine warms to operating temperature. Engine RPM and oil pressure will provide an almost instant reading.

question: Which statement is FALSE regarding inspection of the engine compartment?

answers.text: All v-belts should have the proper amount of tension
The engine and transmission should be free of fluid leaks
The engine should be running during inspection
answers.explanation: The engine should be OFF during the engine compartment inspection so you can move freely without any worry of touching moving parts.

explanation: The engine should be OFF during the engine compartment inspection so you can move freely without any worry of touching moving parts.

question: Which of the following is NOT federally regulated to be carried in every commercial motor vehicle?

answers.explanation: All CMVs are required to carry a fire extinguisher and spare electrical fuses. Plus three reflective triangles, six fuses, or three liquid-burning flares for signaling other drivers when your vehicle is stopped on the side of the road.

explanation: All CMVs are required to carry a fire extinguisher and spare electrical fuses. Plus three reflective triangles, six fuses, or three liquid-burning flares for signaling other drivers when your vehicle is stopped on the side of the road.

question: The primary purpose of a front-end header board is to:

answers.explanation: The primary purpose of the front-end header board is to prevent cargo from moving forward that could injure the driver during a sudden stop

answers.text: allow a mounting area for temporary tanks
form the front wall of the trailer

explanation: The primary purpose of the front-end header board is to prevent cargo from moving forward that could injure the driver during a sudden stop

question: Which statement is true regarding the CDL skills test?

answers.text: The Class A test will always require a full vehicle inspection
Failing a portion of a skills test requires that the entire test be retaken.
answers.explanation: Failing any portion of the skills test will require that the entire test be retaken at the next opportunity.

answers.text: The Class C or B test will be randomly determined from five possible sub sections

explanation: Failing any portion of the skills test will require that the entire test be retaken at the next opportunity.

question: Signs of an exhaust leak include each of the following, EXCEPT

answers.explanation: When inspecting a vehicle for a suspected exhaust leak, check for rusted areas of the exhaust, breaks, cracks, holes, or the presence of carbon soot, a vibration related to engine RPM, and increased exhaust noise.

explanation: When inspecting a vehicle for a suspected exhaust leak, check for rusted areas of the exhaust, breaks, cracks, holes, or the presence of carbon soot, a vibration related to engine RPM, and increased exhaust noise.

question: The inspection of a fuel tank(s) will include all the following, EXCEPT:

answers.text: The line to the overflow bottle is secure
answers.explanation: Overflow bottles are used for the radiator, not the fuel tank. Tanks should be secure, undamaged, and have no leaks. The crossover line should be secure. The tanks should have adequate fuel and the caps should be securely in place.

answers.text: They are securely mounted and not leaking

explanation: Overflow bottles are used for the radiator, not the fuel tank. Tanks should be secure, undamaged, and have no leaks. The crossover line should be secure. The tanks should have adequate fuel and the caps should be securely in place.

question: All the following may be checked at the rear of the trailer, EXCEPT:

answers.explanation: Inspect the physical portion of the trailer parking brake at each axle and/or wheel. Testing the parking brake will be done from inside the cab.

explanation: Inspect the physical portion of the trailer parking brake at each axle and/or wheel. Testing the parking brake will be done from inside the cab.

question: Which of the following should be removed from the cab and kept with you during your walk around inspection of the vehicle?

answers.explanation: Take the ignition key with you during the inspection. This ensures that nobody can start the engine or turn on a system while you are inspecting it while keeping the vehicle secure and under your control.

explanation: Take the ignition key with you during the inspection. This ensures that nobody can start the engine or turn on a system while you are inspecting it while keeping the vehicle secure and under your control.

question: Each of the following is true regarding a vehicle equipped with a cargo lift during the CDL test, EXCEPT:

answers.text: You will need to inspect it for damage and correct operation
You will need to explain the process for checking correct operation
You will need to ensure the lift is fully extended for storage
answers.explanation: If equipped, you will be required to verbally explain the process of checking correct operation while you perform the actions. You must inspect the lift for any damage and make sure it is fully retracted and securely latched.

explanation: If equipped, you will be required to verbally explain the process of checking correct operation while you perform the actions. You must inspect the lift for any damage and make sure it is fully retracted and securely latched.

question: The instrument cluster gauge responsible for indicating whether the alternator/generator is operating correctly is the:

answers.explanation: The alternator/generator provides electrical power for recharging the batteries while supplementing the batteries to run electrical systems when the engine is running. Most gauges provide a voltage reading, but some may provide an amperage reading to indicate that the alternator is generating power.

explanation: The alternator/generator provides electrical power for recharging the batteries while supplementing the batteries to run electrical systems when the engine is running. Most gauges provide a voltage reading, but some may provide an amperage reading to indicate that the alternator is generating power.

question: When performing brake tests, which brake system will be tested first?

answers.explanation: When following the recommended inspection procedures the parking brakes will be tested first followed immediately by the service brakes.

explanation: When following the recommended inspection procedures the parking brakes will be tested first followed immediately by the service brakes.

question: During the engine compartment inspection, the deflection of v-belts should be between:

answers.explanation: Each of the v-belts on the engine should have belt deflection that is between 1/2 and 3/4 inch.

explanation: Each of the v-belts on the engine should have belt deflection that is between 1/2 and 3/4 inch.

question: Excessive steering wheel play in the steering gearbox may cause you to over or understeer. Steering wheel play should be no more than:

answers.explanation: Ideally, the steering wheel play should be 10 degrees or less. This is about 1 and 3/4 inch of play on a 20 inch steering wheel. Typically, this can be adjusted on the steering gearbox.

explanation: Ideally, the steering wheel play should be 10 degrees or less. This is about 1 and 3/4 inch of play on a 20 inch steering wheel. Typically, this can be adjusted on the steering gearbox.

question: Which task is NOT performed during the in-cab inspection?

answers.explanation: Upon entering the cab, ensure that the transmission is in neutral or park. Start the engine and listen for any unusual sounds.

answers.text: Listening for any unusual noises once the engine starts
Ensuring that the transmission is in neutral (manual) or park (automatic)

explanation: Upon entering the cab, ensure that the transmission is in neutral or park. Start the engine and listen for any unusual sounds.

question: If you were to fail one portion of your CDL skills test, what would be the result?

answers.text: Failure of the test and needing to only retake the portion you failed.
Failure of the test and needing to retake the entire test.
answers.explanation: Upon failing a single portion, you will fail the entire test and will then need to retake the entire test at a later time.

answers.text: Failure of the test and not being allowed to re-take the test.

explanation: Upon failing a single portion, you will fail the entire test and will then need to retake the entire test at a later time.

question: Which external lights are the most difficult to check without an assistant during daylight?

answers.explanation: Brake lights can be the most difficult to check on your own, during the daylight. At night it is easy to see the brake lights being reflected off areas behind the vehicle. The trailer hand brake can help to verify that the trailer brake lights are operating because they will remain on until you manually flip the hand brake off.

explanation: Brake lights can be the most difficult to check on your own, during the daylight. At night it is easy to see the brake lights being reflected off areas behind the vehicle. The trailer hand brake can help to verify that the trailer brake lights are operating because they will remain on until you manually flip the hand brake off.

question: During inspection of the engine coolant, you should NOT:

answers.text: Check the level in the radiator by removing the cap of a hot engine
answers.explanation: Never remove the radiator cap of an engine that is up to operating temperature. Touching the upper radiator hose(s) is actually the best indicator of whether the thermostat in the engine is open or not as it will be hot when the coolant is flowing. Use the overflow bottle to check the coolant level or wait for the engine to cool off.

answers.text: Poor cold or room temperature coolant into the overflow bottle with the engine running
Fill the overflow bottle to the HOT level while the engine is warm

explanation: Never remove the radiator cap of an engine that is up to operating temperature. Touching the upper radiator hose(s) is actually the best indicator of whether the thermostat in the engine is open or not as it will be hot when the coolant is flowing. Use the overflow bottle to check the coolant level or wait for the engine to cool off.
